yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday's session exceeded the plan significantly: ran 6.1mi at 142bpm average (59 TSS) when the plan called for 15min easy brick run off the bike. The ROUVY bike session appears to have been skipped entirely (0 TSS recorded). The pool swim was executed well at 1.3mi (19 TSS). Running over-distance this close to race day (11 days out) during taper introduces unnecessary fatigue risk, especially with Perthes hip considerations.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's plan exactly as written: 40min easy ROUVY ride (NP under 140w) + 1,500m easy pool swim. After yesterday's unplanned run volume, discipline and restraint are critical today — stay aerobic, protect the legs, and resist any urge to add intensity or duration.
weekProgress: Week 12 of 13 shows 86 TSS of 200 target (43% complete) with 4 days remaining including today. Given the back-loaded schedule and Mon's brick run deviation, progress is reasonable but requires adherence to remaining sessions. Monday's bike was skipped, which cost ~30 TSS. Thu bike spin, Sat OWS, and Sun easy run remain to complete the taper week.
keyInsight: Taper discipline was compromised yesterday — running 6.1mi instead of the prescribed 15min brick run while skipping the bike session contradicts the taper strategy that worked at Oceanside (conservative pacing = 16min run PR).
